explicit_text: I was born in Klo Pa, Thailand in 1995, but there was very little there, so my family decided to move to a better place for our future. We moved to the Mae La Oon camp in Thailand, where I went to school and lived with my family. Thailand was the country that hosted our refugee camp between Burma and Thailand, and it became my home until we moved to the United States in 2014.
implicit_text: I was born in Klo Pa, a small town in Southeast Asia, in 1995, but there was very little there, so my family decided to move to a better place for our future. We moved to a refugee camp located in the same country, where I went to school and lived with my family. This country hosted the camp between Burma and itself, and it became my home until we moved to the United States in 2014.

explicit_text: Mae La Oon camp in Thailand was where my family and I lived after leaving Klo Pa. I attended Young Ni Oo High School there, which was located in section 13 of the camp. The camp was a place where many Karen people like me lived, and it became my home until we moved to St. Paul, Minnesota in 2014.

explicit_text: I was born in Klo Pa, Thailand in 1995. At that time, there was very little in Klo Pa, so my family decided to move to a new place to find a better life and education. Leaving Klo Pa was the start of our journey that eventually led us to Mae La Oon camp and later to the United States.
implicit_text: I was born in a small town in northern Thailand in 1995. At that time, there was very little there, so my family decided to move to a new place to find a better life and education. Leaving that town was the start of our journey that eventually led us to the refugee camp and later to the United States.

explicit_text: I studied at Young Ni Oo High School inside the Mae La Oon camp. The school was founded in May 2003 and had about 500 students and 22 teachers. It was a wooden building with a leaf roof, and we had classes in subjects like math, English, science, and Karen. I really liked learning English and math there, and the school had a student group called the New Generation Students' Organisation to help us support each other.

I really liked learning English and math there, and the school had a student group called the New Generation Students' Organisation to help us support each other.

explicit_text: In October 2014, my family and I moved to St. Paul, Minnesota in the United States. It was a big change for me because I had not finished school yet, and everything was so different from what I was used to. We faced many challenges since we didn’t know what to expect, but thankfully we were able to connect with relatives here and start a new life.

explicit_text: At Young Ni Oo High School, we had a student team called the New Generation Students' Organisation, or NGSO. This group helped students support each other and organized activities for us. Being part of NGSO was important because it created a sense of community and teamwork among the students in the camp school.

explicit_text: Burma is the country near where I lived in the Mae La Oon camp, which is located between Thailand and Burma. My school taught subjects including Burmese and Karen languages, reflecting our cultural ties to Burma. Although I lived in Thailand, my identity as a Karen is deeply connected to Burma, and this influenced my education and community life.
implicit_text: The country bordering the refugee camp where I lived is located next to northern Thailand. My school taught subjects including the languages of that country and Karen, reflecting our cultural ties there. Although I lived in Thailand, my identity as a Karen is deeply connected to that neighboring country, and this influenced my education and community life.

explicit_text: My family and I moved to the United States in October 2014, settling in St. Paul, Minnesota. Coming to the U.S. was a huge change because I hadn’t finished school yet and everything was so different from what I knew in Thailand. Despite the challenges and uncertainties during our long trip, I am grateful to be here and hope to one day return to help students back at my old school.
